items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance telling states to prepare for a statutory change that limits when states can claim federal matching funds (FFP) for Medicaid and CHIP coverage for people who are not U.S. citizens or nationals (or who are in certain noncitizen categories). The change is scheduled to begin October 1, 2026, and CMS points states to an accompanying State Health Official letter for operational steps tied to section 71109 of Public Law 119-21.

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2 for many early filers
items.summary: The IRS said it opened the 2026 filing season on January 26, 2026, and listed April 15 as the federal tax filing deadline for tax year 2025 returns. For taxpayers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC), the IRS expects many refunds to be available by March 2, 2026 (direct deposit, no other issues), and “Where’s My Refund?” is expected to show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: USDA lists SNAP food-restriction waiver rollouts (e.g., Florida 04/20/26; Texas 04/01/26; Virginia 10/01/26)
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service posted a state-by-state list of approved SNAP food-restriction waivers that restrict purchases like soda, candy, energy drinks, and certain desserts, with target implementation dates that vary by state. Examples on the list include Florida (04/20/26) restricting soda/energy drinks/candy/prepared desserts, Texas (04/01/26) restricting sweetened drinks and candy, and Virginia (10/01/26) restricting “sweetened beverages.”

items.title: USDA seeks comment on SNAP-Ed reporting as required funding ends after FY 2025 allocation; comments due May 20, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s FNS published a notice renewing SNAP-Ed reporting requirements so states can submit final SNAP-Ed annual reports ahead of program closeout after Congress ended required SNAP-Ed funding with the FY 2025 grant allocation (Section 10107 of Public Law 119-21). The notice requests public comment, with comments due May 20, 2026.

items.title: HUD changes Emergency Housing Voucher/Stability Voucher rules: removes prior SSN/citizenship verification waiver; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD issued Notice PIH 2026-05 revising Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) requirements by striking the earlier waiver that allowed admission without SSN or eligible immigration documentation (with a 180-day post-admission deadline). HUD directs PHAs to follow 24 CFR 5.508 (citizenship/eligible immigration status) and 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 (SSNs) and says PHAs must be in compliance within 60 days of the notice publication for new SV admissions and current EHV/SV families.

items.title: HUD rescinds streamlined waiver process for admitting people experiencing homelessness to HCV/PBV/public housing
items.summary: HUD issued Notice PIH 2026-04 rescinding Notice PIH 2024-17, which had provided a streamlined review process for regulatory waivers to expedite admission for people experiencing homelessness in the Housing Choice Voucher (HCV), Project-Based Voucher (PBV), and public housing programs. HUD notes that waivers already approved under the old process remain valid until they expire under their terms.

items.title: USDA: WIC food package updates — states must implement most provisions within 24 months; higher fruit/veg benefit within 60 days
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food-package Q&A says state agencies must implement the final-rule provisions within 24 months of publication (with some exceptions). The Q&A also says increased cash-value benefits (the fruit/vegetable benefit) must be implemented no later than 60 days from publication of the final rule.

items.title: SSA Red Book: SSI federal benefit rate is $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (couple) for 2026
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 update in the Red Book lists the 2026 SSI federal benefit rate (FBR) as $994 per month for an eligible individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple. The same page also lists 2026 disability work-incentive thresholds, including Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) at $1,690/month for most disabled workers and $2,830/month for blind individuals.

items.title: KFF tracker: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment was 75.7 million as of Dec. 2025 (down from March 2023 peak)
items.summary: KFF’s Medicaid Enrollment and Unwinding Tracker reports 75.7 million people enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP nationally as of December 2025, down 20% from the March 2023 peak of 94 million. The tracker also notes enrollment remained 6% higher than February 2020 levels, highlighting how coverage has shifted after the continuous coverage period ended.

items.title: KFF: Medicaid work requirements guidance/implementation is a key 2026 watch item; federal deadline cited as Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: KFF’s “What to Watch in 2026” brief flags implementation of Medicaid work requirements and related eligibility changes in the 2025 reconciliation law as a major 2026 issue for states. The brief points to a January 1, 2027 start date for work requirements in the law and notes some states may move sooner (for example, Nebraska is cited as enforcing work requirements early starting May 1, 2026).

items.title: Federal Register: proposed reallotment of FY 2025 LIHEAP funds; comments due Apr. 30, 2026
items.summary: The Administration for Children and Families (OCS) announced a proposed reallotment of federal fiscal year 2025 LIHEAP funds that are available for reallocation to eligible recipients. The Federal Register notice lists April 30, 2026 as the deadline to submit comments on the proposal.
